summary: R3 fixes are implemented. Existing same-district postwar arrangements are now rejected across victor polities by treating any non-local administrative district marker as an occupied postwar arrangement for that district. The QA restore-vassal-ruler by victor 1 followed by direct-control by victor 3 path now rejects before mutating controller, suzerain, obligations, enfeoffments, or administrative markers.

tests_run.result: R3 red pass before implementation: new different-victor same-district regression failed because the second direct-control command was accepted.
Formatted the touched R3 implementation and test files.
Targeted postwar file passed after R3 fix: 1 file passed; 9 tests passed.
sim-core package tests passed: 14 files passed; 95 tests passed.
protocol package tests passed: 1 file passed; 7 tests passed.

Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root Vitest 24 files/155 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.
Workspace typecheck passed for all packages with typecheck scripts.

tests_run.result: Task JSON parsed and confirmed status REVIEW, route_to systems_architect, risk_level R3, and scoped allowed/forbidden paths.

tests_run.result: All 17 changed or untracked paths reported by git status are within allowed_paths and none match forbidden_paths.
No Math.random, Date.now, performance.now, new Date, React, Pixi, Electron, or fs runtime API matches in touched implementation/test files.

No marching, battle, campaign, siege, supply, reinforcement, full diplomacy, treaty, legitimacy, rebellion, client UI, content-source, or forbidden client package scope matches in touched implementation/test files.
Worktree includes task-scoped implementation/test/task changes plus task-scoped orchestration message/route files. No forbidden-path changes.

decisions: R3 conflict rule: reject any postwar arrangement command when the target district already has a non-local administrative district marker, regardless of incoming victorPolityId or settlementId.
No replacement semantics were added. Retiring prior suzerain edges, obligations, and enfeoffments would be a broader treaty/replacement model and remains out of M3 scope.
The R2 same-victor protections remain intact: duplicate arrangements reject, direct-after-vassal rejects, and restore-vassal-ruler still requires concrete ruler and policy fields.
The new regression fixture includes a third polity and uses matching actor authority for victor 3 so the rejection proves the district-scoped postwar guard, not an authority failure.
The different-victor regression asserts rejection, unchanged state hash, unchanged district controller, no victor 3 administrative marker, and no victor 3 obligations.
risks: R3 public protocol surface remains changed from the original M3 implementation and still needs systems_architect review for long-term command/query naming and compatibility.

The explicit replacement/clearing path is intentionally not implemented. If design later needs postwar arrangement replacement, systems_architect should define deterministic retirement events and rights-clearing semantics before implementation.
Projection coefficients remain prototype constants for M3 validation and are not final balance.
requested_action: Review the R3 district-scoped cross-victor rejection rule and regression coverage, then route to QA if systems review accepts.
